preamble: The transformation from a nasalized close front unrounded vowel /ĩ/ to a palatalized voiced alveolar nasal /nʲ/ is an attested historical sound change, often involving the reinterpretation of vowel nasality and the development of a consonantal segment, frequently influenced by adjacent sounds or the loss of a preceding nasal consonant.

examples.note: From Latin 'vinum', the intermediate stage 'vio' would have contained a nasalized vowel /ĩ/ which developed into /nʲ/.

sources: Etxeberria, U. (2014). Different distributions of contrastive vowel nasalization in Basque. Loquens, 1(1), 1-24.
Lipski, J. M. (1994). Latin n in Portuguese: The case of vinho. In Miscelánea de estudios en homenaje a Antonio Tovar (pp. 245-254). Universidad de Salamanca.
